name: wifi_monitor
description: LAN host discovery, async port scanning, device fingerprinting, CVE lookup via CIRCL, and threat detection
author: 0xb0rn3 | github.com/0xb0rn3

optional_dependencies.scapy: ARP host discovery — pip3 install scapy (requires root)
optional_dependencies.aiohttp: Async CVE lookup via cve.circl.lu — pip3 install aiohttp

help.description: Discovers live hosts on a LAN using ARP (scapy) with TCP-ping fallback, then async-scans open ports, grabs service banners, and looks up CVEs via the CIRCL API. Fingerprints device type and OS from port/banner patterns. Flags threats: exposed databases, cleartext protocols, legacy SSH versions.

ports.description: Ports to scan — comma-separated or range (e.g. '22,80,443' or '1-1024'). 'default' uses a common 23-port set.

port_scan.description: Run port scan on discovered hosts

cve_lookup.description: Look up CVEs via CIRCL API based on service banners

concurrency.description: Concurrent port probe workers per host

examples.description: Scan local /24 for live hosts and open ports
Scan single host with custom port range
Host discovery only, no port scan

features: ARP host discovery via scapy (requires root) with TCP-ping fallback
Async port scanning with banner grabbing (HTTP, HTTPS, SSH, generic)
Device fingerprinting: IoT camera, router, NAS, DB server, web server
CVE lookup via CIRCL cve.circl.lu API with 24-hour cache
Threat detection: exposed databases, cleartext protocols, legacy SSH
Works without scapy/aiohttp at reduced capability

installation_tiers.basic: TCP-ping discovery only — no extra deps required
installation_tiers.standard: ARP discovery — requires scapy (pip3 install scapy, run as root)
installation_tiers.full: CVE enrichment — requires aiohttp (pip3 install aiohttp)

notes: AUTHORIZATION REQUIRED: Only scan networks you own or have explicit permission to test
ARP scan requires root/sudo for raw socket access
CVE lookup requires internet access to cve.circl.lu
Large /16 or /8 subnets will be slow — use /24 or smaller
